chapter_title.ar: باب بِنَاءِ الْمَسَاجِدِ وَ مَا يُؤْخَذُ مِنْهَا وَ الْحَدَثِ فِيهَا مِنَ النَّوْمِ وَ غَيْرِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 48 – Construction of the Masjid, and what can be taken from it, and the breaking of the ablution inside it due to sleep or something else

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/248)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/248)
text: قُلْتُ لَهُ الرَّجُلُ يَكُونُ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ فِي الصَّلَاةِ فَيُرِيدُ أَنْ يَبْزُقَ فَقَالَ عَنْ يَسَارِهِ وَ إِنْ كَانَ فِي غَيْرِ صَلَاةٍ فَلَا يَبْزُقُ حِذَاءَ الْقِبْلَةِ وَ يَبْزُقُ عَنْ يَمِينِهِ وَ يَسَارِهِ .

en.hubeali: A group, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Husayn Bin Saeed, from Muhammad Bin Mihran Al Karkhy, from Abdullah Bin Sinan,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws, said, ‘I said to himasws, ‘The man happens to be in the Masjid in the Salāt, so he wants to spit’. So heasws said: ‘On his left. And if he was in other than a Salāt, so he should not spit facing the Qiblah, and he can spit on his right and his left’.
en.sarwar: 12. A group has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Husayn ibn Sa‘id from Muhammad ibn Mehran al-Karkhiy from ‘Abd Allah ibn Sinan who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who is in Masjid in Salah (prayer) and wants to spit away. He (the Imam) said, ‘He can do it toward his left and if he is not in Salah (prayer); he must not do so in front of al-Qiblah (al-Ka‘bah) but do so toward his left or right sides.’”
